reasons: sweep_reversal and bos_continuation both show genuine alpha on 2 years of real FX data (Sharpe >1.4)
sweep_reversal was dead on synthetic data due to a wiring bug (detect_sweeps never called), now fixed and producing Sharpe 1.528
fvg_retrace confirmed harmful on real data (Sharpe -0.184), should be permanently removed
execution fragility is 0% for all top candidates — alpha survives realistic costs
all baselines dramatically underperform, confirming SMC edge is real
drawdown of 36% blocks deployment gate — this is a risk management issue, not a strategy issue
reducing position size from 0.5% to 0.25% per trade should mechanically reduce drawdown to ~18%
blocking_issues: max_drawdown 36-37% exceeds 21% gate threshold — requires risk management tightening
no holdout evaluation possible at current drawdown level
no spread data in yfinance — using fixed 1.5 pip assumption
open_questions: will tighter position sizing (0.25%) reduce drawdown below gate threshold while preserving Sharpe?
does M15 execution (60-day real data) produce a different drawdown profile?
should sweep_reversal or bos_continuation be the primary family if forced to choose one?
next_actions: tighten risk: base_risk_per_trade 0.5% -> 0.25%, add 15% max drawdown circuit breaker
re-run campaign with tightened risk parameters
if drawdown passes gate, run holdout evaluation on shortlisted candidates
freeze sweep_plus_bos as promoted champion candidate
investigate M15 real data (60 days) as supplementary validation
